responsive_strategy.approach: Mobile-first single-column. At md (768) most multi-card sections shift to 2-col grids; at lg (1024) they finalize to 2-col (Gain), 2-col (Syllabus), grid-of-4 (Instructors row), 3-col (LeadMore / CtaCards). Hero rewires from stacked (mobile) to 2-col text+image (desktop). Tabs become a sticky horizontal nav at lg with full-width backdrop-blur bar. PreRequisites mobile is a simple checklist; desktop adds decorative tilted images flanking the content. CtaCards (3 light cards) appear only in the mobile authored frame — desktop closes with the dark CTA + footer alone. Render CtaCards via `lg:hidden` OR move desktop sibling content into the same component and gate with a `variant` prop.
responsive_strategy.token_rule: All colors via Tailwind tokens from apps/web/src/app/globals.css (bg-surface-*, text-text-*, border-border-*). Typography via text-h1..h5 / text-mini. NO inline hex, NO clamp().
responsive_strategy.reuse_strategy: Nav and Footer reuse existing apps/web/src/components/site-header.tsx and (if present) site-footer / footer component. Codebase-scout will verify exact paths.

shared_data_modules.shape: { slug, breadcrumb, title, subtitle, body, meta: {schedule,location,time,language}, gainHighlights: GainItem[6], syllabus: SyllabusItem[6], preRequisites: string[5], faqs: FAQ[4], instructors: Instructor[4], relatedCourses: Course[3] }

Stack: breadcrumb -> title (40px) -> body (14px) -> 4 pill chips wrap-flex -> CTA pill -> 240px image rectangle below CTA. DESKTOP: two columns at left-[309px] right-padding-matching (max-w ~1304). Left col w-[638]: breadcrumb -> title (68px) -> body -> row of 4 SQUARE meta cards (110x104, white card + vector icon + 14px label). Right col: large hero image (~600x600). components.notes: Hero background bg-surface-brand-soft on mobile is full-width; desktop spans full 1924px. Title second line ('Course') uses regular weight in mobile but stays brand-green in both.
Desktop variant = 110x104 white card with rounded-[17px], opacity-82. Mobile variant = inline pill chip with border. Use a `variant: 'pill' | 'card'` prop OR responsive class swaps.

sections.responsive: MOBILE: full-width white bar with thin border, 5 pill tabs left-aligned (overflow-x-auto if needed). DESKTOP: 1924px-wide sticky bar at y=900 (right under hero) with backdrop-blur and centered/left-padded tab row (gap-[41px]). Use `sticky top-0` once header is scrolled past, or non-sticky absolute as authored — visual-QA decides.

props.title: Elevate Your Practice — Literally
body: Master the art of yoga in the air with our Aerial Yoga Teacher Training Course at Bodhi School of Yoga. Build strength, grace, and confidence while learning to teach this unique practice.

You'll combine traditional yoga postures with graceful aerial movements — learning how inversions decompress the spine, build core stability, and create a deeply healing experience for your future students.

sections.purpose: 'What You'll Gain' — 6 highlight cards each with circular icon, bold title, body description.
sections.responsive: MOBILE: single-column stack of 6 cards (each 342w, bg-surface-soft, border, rounded-[12px], p-[16px]). DESKTOP: 2-column grid of 3 rows (cards 641x151–159, white bg, drop-shadow, rounded-[19px], col gap 18px, row gap 15px). Card width grows on desktop; icon moves to slightly different position.

sections.purpose: 'Frequently Asked Questions' accordion — 4 items, first one expanded by default with body copy visible. Border-bottom dividers between items.
sections.responsive: MOBILE: full-width 24px-padded. DESKTOP: centered max-w 910px at left-[314px]. Same accordion behavior. Eyebrow 'FAQ'.

items.q: Do I need prior experience in aerial yoga?
items.a: No prior aerial yoga experience is required. A basic understanding of yoga postures and physical fitness to perform inversions and aerial movements is recommended.

items.q: Is this TTC Yoga Alliance certified?
items.a: Yes — Bodhi is a Yoga Alliance Registered School (RYS-200, RYS-300). Course certification details on enquiry.

items.q: Will I get to practice on hammocks during training?
items.a: Yes. Every session is hands-on; you'll spend significant time practising on the aerial hammock under supervision.

items.q: Can this course help me start my own aerial yoga classes?
items.a: Yes — the syllabus includes a dedicated 'Business of Aerial Yoga' module covering branding, pricing, and launching classes.

components.notes: Accordion answers for items 2–4 are inferred (Figma shows only collapsed titles). Builder may stub or fetch real content from CMS later.
